introSection: Carolina Cyclone is a steel roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. Opened on March 22, 1980, it was built by Arrow Development and was the first coaster to feature four inversions, as well as being the first coaster with the double loop corkscrew layout.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On June 21, 1979, Carowinds announced the addition of the first roller coaster with four inversions.

Carolina Cyclone opened on March 22, 1980.

The ride was repainted with multiple different colors for the 2010 season.

In 2020, the outer shells of the trains were replaced with ones from Vortex at Kings Island, which closed in 2019. As Carowinds would not open at all in 2020, the refurbished trains debuted in 2021.

During the 2021-2022 off-season, Carolina Cyclone was once again repainted.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Park guests can walk underneath the double corkscrew.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Teal track and white supports. The ride previously featured blue track on the final brake run, first turn and lift hill, with orange track on the rest of the track, and yellow on the inversions, with the supports being blue. The ride's color scheme before that featured blue track and black supports.

fullText:
Carolina Cyclone is a steel roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. Opened on March 22, 1980, it was built by Arrow Development and was the first coaster to feature four inversions, as well as being the first coaster with the double loop corkscrew layout.

On June 21, 1979, Carowinds announced the addition of the first roller coaster with four inversions.

Carolina Cyclone opened on March 22, 1980.

The ride was repainted with multiple different colors for the 2010 season.

In 2020, the outer shells of the trains were replaced with ones from Vortex at Kings Island, which closed in 2019. As Carowinds would not open at all in 2020, the refurbished trains debuted in 2021.

During the 2021-2022 off-season, Carolina Cyclone was once again repainted.

Park guests can walk underneath the double corkscrew.

Teal track and white supports. The ride previously featured blue track on the final brake run, first turn and lift hill, with orange track on the rest of the track, and yellow on the inversions, with the supports being blue. The ride's color scheme before that featured blue track and black supports.
